meaning: n.煽动暴乱,骚乱,煽动性的言论(或行为)
examples: The only major objection came from Justice Antonin Scalia, who offered an even more robust defense of state privileges going back to the Alien and Sedition Acts.
They sowed sedition broadcast over the country.
A former university lecturer has also been arrested for sedition.
Rights activists have long criticized Malaysia's anti-sedition law as a tool to curb democratic dissent.
The outspoken Malaysian opposition MP was fined last month for disseminating false news and committing sedition.
